2000-09-01.title: Long Nomads, the Inuit Find a Settled Life Unsettling
2000-09-01.abstract: Canada's Inuit in new northern territory of Nunavut are having rocky cultural transition from nomadic existence to modern, sedentary life of snowmobiles, heated homes and satellite television; map; one price is Canada's highest rate of violent crime, particularly violence against women; Levi Barnabas, former speaker of legislature, has been convicted of sexual assault, and education minister James Arvaluk is charged with assaulting woman (M)

2000-09-01.title: Somalis Get Leader; Now They Need a Nation
2000-09-01.abstract: MOGADISHU, Somalia, Aug. 30 — Somalia finally has a government, and its first president in nearly a decade arrived here today escorted by at least 120 pickup trucks carrying more than 1,000 militiamen armed with rifles, rocket launchers, even double-barreled antiaircraft guns.

2000-09-01.title: Chinese Leader Sued in New York Over Deaths Stemming From Tiananmen Crackdown
2000-09-01.abstract: Five veterans of 1989 Tiananmen Square democracy movement sue Chinese leader Li Peng in federal court in New York for human rights abuses stemming from his role in military crackdown; photo; Li, in New York for UN parliamentary conference, is served with court summons at Waldorf Towers (M)

2000-09-01.title: For Iran's Visiting Legislators, A Useful, Low-Key Exchange
2000-09-01.abstract: Iranian legislators in New York for UN parliament meeting attend Metropolitan Museum reception and talk with Sen Arlen Specter, Repr Bob Ney, Repr Gary Ackerman and other Americans; issue of Iranian Jews convicted of espionage noted; Mouris Motamed, sole Jewish member of parliament, says Iranian Jewsare living as well as Muslim neighbors (M)

2000-09-01.title: Russia Identifies U.S. Sub
2000-09-01.abstract: Russian news agency identifies second US submarine in Barents Sea when submarine Kursk sank as the Toledo (S)

2000-09-02.title: European Leaders Praise U.S. Antimissile Decision
2000-09-02.abstract: European leaders praise Pres Clinton's decision to defer building missile defense shield over US; Russian officials say their diplomatic campaign against US program helped persuade Clinton (M)

2000-09-02.title: A Key Runway Inspection Was Skipped the Day of the Concorde Crash, Investigators Report
2000-09-02.abstract: A Key Runway Inspection Was Skipped the Day

2000-09-02.title: Israel Unrattled by Iraq, but U.S. Unit Alerted
2000-09-02.abstract: Prime Min Ehud Barak displays no an

2000-09-02.title: Antimissile Defenses: Clinton Decides to Keep U.S. Options Open
2000-09-02.abstract: Jane Perlez analysis holds that Pres Clinton, in declaring that his administration would not deploy national missile defense, made his case that US could pursue arms control agenda but must also explore ways to meet post-cold war missile threat (M)

2000-09-02.title: Palestinians Convict and Sentence Terrorist
2000-09-02.abstract: Palestinian court in Nablus sentences Mahmoud Abu Hanoud, Islamic militant whom Israelis failed to capture in botched raid on West Bank house, to 12 years in prison (S)

2000-09-03.title: Lawyers for Ecuador Indians See U.S. Judge Linked to Texaco
2000-09-03.abstract: Federal judicial and tax documents show that Federal Judge Jed S Rakoff, who for four years had been considering whether to hear lawsuit filed against Texaco by Ecuadorean Indians who say oil company polluted their lands, attended an expenses-paid seminar on environmental issues held by foundation receiving regular donations from company; former chief exec of Texaco spoke at conference; lawyers for Indians, saying there is appearance of conflict of interest, file motion with Rakoff requesting that he recuse himself and turn case over to someone else (M)

2000-09-03.title: Near Beijing, Three Bears Find Rescue And Refuge
2000-09-03.abstract: Beijing (China) Forestry Bureau officers raid restaurant and rescue black bears that restaurant had been keeping in tiny cages, milking bears' gall bladders of bile to sell for its reputed medicinal value; bureau acts after sensational account of situation in Beijing Morning Post; bears are taken to Beijing Wildlife Rescue Center; are still recovering from their ordeal and may wind up in special reserve or zoo, in an episode that reflects changing attitudes about animal welfare among China's emerging middle class (M)

2000-09-03.title: A Feeble Croatia Is Battling Its Ex-Leaders
2000-09-03.abstract: Croatia Pres Stipe Mesic's first seven months in power have been sobering; he has been determined to bring Croatia into European Union, but economy he inherited afetr death of former Pres Franjo Tudjman is in seriously bad shape; he was elected promising an end to corruption and nepotism of Tudjman government, but court system used for years as political tool has not been strong enough or independent enough to prosecute law vigorously; levels of corruption Mesic has uncovered from old government have far outstripped his worst imaginings, running into the billions of dollars; photo (L)

2000-09-03.title: Burma Leader Forced Home; Standoff Ends
2000-09-03.abstract: Aide to Daw Aung San Suu Kyi, Myanmar's pro-democracy leader, says that in sudden midnight raid, nearly 200 riot police surround car where Aung San Suu Kyi had been stranded by roadside for a week and force her and her companions to return home; Western diplomat says that security forces then seal off political headquarters of Aung San Suu Kyi and apparently search it and remove documents; forceful action brings to abrupt end an attempt by Aung San Suu Kyi to publicize restrictions on her political movement (M)

2000-09-03.title: Lebanon Challenger Takes On Syria, Too
2000-09-03.abstract: Lebanon is slipping toward constitutional crisis--and a tough early test for Syria's new young leader, Bashar al-Assad--as it goes into final round of parliamentary elections on Sept 3; Rafik Hariri, flamboyant multibillionaire real estate developer, appears poised to roll up majority in 128-seat Parliament in his effort to come back as prime minister; but Pres Emile Lahoud, annointed by Syria, which for decade has been only real political power in Beirut, appears determined to block Hariri from regaining office he held from 1992 to 1998; photo of Hariri; map (L)

2000-09-04.title: With Clinton Due, Holbrooke Defends U.N.
2000-09-04.abstract: Richard C Holbrooke, American ambassador to United Nations, says US must focus more on its ties with UN, which he says is indispensable to American foreign policy; does not disagree that reform is still needed at UN and says he has been dismayed by bureaucratic behavior and obstructionism of it international civil servants (M)

2000-09-04.title: U.S. and Russia Explore Joint Project to Dispose of Nuclear Fuel From Russian Subs
2000-09-04.abstract: Energy Sec Bill Richardson tours a secret nuclear submarine base at Petropavlovsk, on Kamchatka Peninsula, and says he would support a Russian request for help in building a high-security storage site for nuclear fuel from decommissioned Russian subs; cites dangers of terrorist theft of reactor fuels and so-called insider sales by Russians desperate for money; Russian Navy wants US to assume half the cost of building $200 million floating dry-dock that would store radioactive reactor casings and nuclear fuel (M)

2000-09-04.title: Opposition Leader Scores Upset in Lebanon Elections
2000-09-04.abstract: Former Prime Min Rafik Hariri deals a crushing electoral blow to Lebanon's Syrian-appointed government in final parliamentary voting, sweeping all of Beirut's seats and gaining a powerful majority of backers in Parliament; Prime Min Salim al-Hoss, his Sunni Muslim rival, and Tamman Salam, himself a former prime minister, are swept away in landslide; election drastically alters political status quo while not eliminating Syria's hold on Lebanon; photo (M)

2000-09-04.title: Milosevic Foe Leading Field In Yugoslavia
2000-09-04.abstract: Vojislav Kostunica, a moderate Serb nationalist, anti-Communist and democrat, is mounting serious campaign to unseat Slobodan Milosevic as Yugoslav president, and polls suggest that he may be able to win; Milosevic is fighting for his political survival after calling elections nine months early in an effort to shore up his international legitimacy; Kostunica expects Milosevic to do everything he can, including fraud, to win first round; photo (M)

2000-09-04.title: In a Divided Israel, Thousands Rally for the Ex-Shas Party Leader as He Goes to Jail
2000-09-04.abstract: Rabbi Aryeh Deri, former leader of Israel's Shas Party, begins three-year sentence for corruption, cheered by thousands of supporters at the prison in Ramle; photo (M)

2000-09-05.title: Metal Piece in Concorde Crash May Be From Continental DC-10
2000-09-05.abstract: Metal strip investigators believe may have led to fiery crash of Air France Concorde in July apparently came from Continental Airlines DC-10 that had taken off from same runway minutes before (S)

2000-09-05.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-09-05.abstract: EUROPE    BRITAIN: LAWMAKER TO QUIT -- Mo Mowlam, the former Northern Ireland secretary whose popularity among the Labor Party faithful and with the public has regularly exceeded that of Prime Minister Tony Blair, said she would not run for re-election to Parliament in the vote expected later this year. Reported to be unhappy at being replaced in the Ulster job in 1999 and given a marginal cabinet position, she said, ''I have several years of my working life left and want to do something different before I finally retire.''   Warren Hoge (NYT)

2000-09-05.title: Lebanese Surprise: Syria's Man Loses by Landslide
2000-09-05.abstract: News analysis of landslide defeat of Syrian-appointed government by billionaire builder Rafik Hariri in Lebanese elections, changing political map of Lebanon overnight; most intriguing aspect of sudden shift is why Syria's new leader Bashar al-Assad allowed it to happen; photo (M)

2000-09-05.title: CLINTON PLANNING DIPLOMATIC WHIRL ON NEW YORK TRIP
2000-09-05.abstract: Pres Clinton reportedly plans diplomatic whirlwind in New York City over next three days while attending opening session of UN General Assembly; will make effort to jump-start failed talks between Israel and Palestinians; may confront China's Pres Jiang Zemin over Beijing's continued missile sales to Pakistan; will announce American support for plan to strengthen UN's peacekeeping department; will use assembly session to insert himself into array of simmering world conflicts; activities represent one of his last opportunities for major foreign policy accomplishments in waning months in office; photos (M)

2000-09-05.title: Case of Joined Twins Moves to England's Court of Appeal
2000-09-05.abstract: England's Court of Appeal hears request to decide whether doctors can proceed with operation to separate joined twins that would sacrifice life of one to save other; parents have objected to plan, even though doctors say both will probably die if surgery is not performed; are seeking to overturn August 25 High Court decision that gave surgeons permission to operate; infants share common heart (M)

2000-09-06.title: Beijing Journal; For a Prize That's Olympian, China Jumps the Gun
2000-09-06.abstract: Beijing, China, is preparing for Summer Olympics of 2008, to which capital desperately wants to play host, even though International Olympic Committee will not select venue for another year; Chinese suffered huge loss of face in 1993 after Sydney, Australia, was selected by slimmest of margins for 2000 Summer Games; photo (M)

2000-09-06.title: Annan Says All Nations Must Cooperate to Solve Problems Like War and Poverty
2000-09-06.abstract: UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan addresses world leaders at General Assembly's Millennium summit in New York; warns perennial problems of war, disease, poverty and pollution will never be resolved unless all countries cooperate; stresses that neither they nor UN can accomplish tasks unaided; calls gathering 'defining moment' for world's leaders and world body; photo (M)

2000-09-06.title: Pakistani Leader Voices Little Hope for India Talks
2000-09-06.abstract: Interview with Gen Pervez Musharraf, Pakistan's military ruler, who says there is no hope of solving explosive issue of Kashmir through negotiations with India; will appeal to world leaders at UN General Assembly's Millennium summit in New York for type of international mediation that ended Indonesian occupation of East Timor; photo (M)

2000-09-06.title: Pentagon Security Gate Misfires, Injuring German
2000-09-06.abstract: Germany's defense minister Rudolf Scharping is injured and briefly hospitalized after retractable steel security barrier springs up beneath motorcade on arrival at honors ceremony at Pentagon; German Embassy attache and American security aide are also injured; other similar incidents recalled (S)

2000-09-06.title: President of Iran Praises His Country's Civilization
2000-09-06.abstract: Iranian Pres Mohammad Khatami addresses UN General Assembly's Millennium summit in New York; promotes his campaign for global dialogue among civilizations; praises his country as one of world's oldest; warns against dangers of unlimited globalization; is applauded by Sec of State Madeleine K Albright, among dignitaries in attendance; photos (M)

2000-09-07.title: Judge Keeps Texaco Case
2000-09-07.abstract: Federal Judge Jed S Rakoff of Manhattan rejects motion filed by lawyers for Ecuadorean Indians requesting that he withdraw from handling their seven-year-old lawsuit against Texaco Inc which accuses Texaco of polluting Indians' Amazon lands; lawyers had cited judge's attendance at expenses-paid seminar at which former Texaco chairman was speaker (S)

2000-09-07.title: The State of the World, and the World to Be, in 149 Chapters
2000-09-07.abstract: United Nations Secretary General Kofi Annan tells 149 world leaders attending 'millennium summit' that they have ability to transform lives of six billion people; each leader is allowed five-minute speech, but meeting will probably be remembered more for private meetings in hotel suites and diplomatic offices around New York City than for anything said in cavernous hall of General Assembly; photo (M)

2000-09-07.title: Elections Near, Rivalries Split Opposition to Milosevic
2000-09-07.abstract: Split in opposition to Pres Slobodan Milosevic puts at risk its main achievement so far: its control over Serbia's major cities, including radio and television stations; American officials press opposition politicians to settle their differences to present united slate of candidates for local elections Sept 24 (M)

2000-09-07.title: Turkish Police Bar Gay Americans From Visiting Aegean Port
2000-09-07.abstract: Turkish police bar several hundred gay Americans from visiting tourist sites near port of Kusadasi and orders them back aboard their cruise ship; this prompts complaints from State Dept; most of tourists are on what travel operators say is largest gay cruise to visit Turkey (M)

2000-09-07.title: 4 Warlords Won´t Accept Somali Leader
2000-09-07.abstract: SANA, Yemen, Sept. 2 — Leaving this capital after a four-day visit, four Somali warlords today warned foreign nations not to recognize the election of a new president in their country, saying it could lead to civil war.

2000-09-08.title: Splendid Maya Palace Is Found Hidden in Jungle
2000-09-08.abstract: Archaeologists uncover ruins of vast and splendid Mayan city in remote Guatemalan jungle; photo; map; diagram; eighth-century palace at Cancuen had 170 high-ceilinged rooms built around 11 courtyards; Dr Arthur A Demarest of Vanderbilt University says earlier expeditions underestimated grandeur of palace and city that was prosperous center of commerce and crafts on Pasion River; Dr David Freidel explains importance of site; expedition co-leader is Tomas Barrientos (M)

2000-09-08.title: LEADERS DEBATE BROAD NEW ROLE FOR U.N. COUNCIL
2000-09-08.abstract: UN Security Council meets with 14 heads of government, including Pres Clinton, and one foreign minister gathered around table; photo; topic of peacekeeping, especially in Africa, expands to discussion about causes of war in age when ethnic cleansing, rape and mutilation have become tactical hallmarks of pickup armies that terrorize populations weakened by poverty and disease; Prime Min Tony Blair calls for broader concept on dealing with such security issues; Clinton warns there will be no real peace until nations confront 'iron link' between deprivation, disease and war; other leaders concur, except for Pres Jiang Zemin of China, who argues stonily for restrictive definition of UN role, and Pres Vladimir Putin of Russia, who is more preoccupied with opposing use of force outside Council's control; Sec Gen Kofi Annan and other leaders comment (M)

2000-09-08.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-09-08.abstract: EUROPE    AUSTRIA: E.U. REPORT DUE  A report commissioned by the European Union to help it decide whether to continue sanctions against Austria is to be presented today to the current holder of the rotating E.U. presidency, President Jacques Chirac of France. The sanctions were imposed in February after the far-right Freedom Party joined Austria's ruling coalition. The report was to evaluate the ''respect'' of the Austrian government for ''common European values'' as well as the evolution of the Freedom Party.    Suzanne Daley (NYT)

2000-09-08.title: Clinton Won't Meet His Goal Of Mideast Peace, Aides Fear
2000-09-08.abstract: White House, having made sustained effort to seal Middle East peace accord, has begun to sense that solution is slipping away, and will not come during Pres Clinton's term; Clinton has met with Prime Min Ehud Barak and Yasir Arafat during UN summit, but Arafat reportedly failed to give assurances that he will agree to solution on Jerusalem; private assessments are gloomy, although neither Barak nor Arafat have threatened to walk away; US official notes that Arafat denies basic respect to negotiating partner when he refuses even to recognize existence of Temple Mount that is most sacred of all places to Jews; Barak reiterates that Jerusalem and Temple Mount are cornerstone of Jewish identity; photo; reminds Arafat that when Jesus walked in Jerusalem he saw not churches or mosques but the Jewish Temple (M)

2000-09-08.title: U.N. Speakers Urge Increase In Charity To the Poor
2000-09-08.abstract: Succession of leaders speaking on second day of UN Millennium Summit call for industrialized world to show more tangible charity toward world's poor, although few speakers offer specific remedies beyond debt forgiveness; South African Thabo Mbeki, Venezuelan Pres Hugo Chavez, Italian Prime Min Giuliano Amato, Canadian Prime Min Jean Chretien and others quoted (M)

2000-09-09.title: India and Pakistan Continue War of Words, Minus Names, Over Kashmir
2000-09-09.abstract: Prime Min Atal Bihari Vajpayee of India responds to speech by Gen Pervez Musharraf of Pakistan at United Nations Millennium Summit attacking India's occupation of Kashmir with counterattack that does not name Pakistan (M)

2000-09-09.title: Local Scandals Govern Hong Kong Vote
2000-09-09.abstract: Most Hong Kong voters see legislative elections as chance to register dissatisfaction with local establishment, not to stand up to China; focus has shifted from debates about freedom and law to local scandals and economic problems (M)

2000-09-09.title: Clinton and China Leader Meet but With Little Gain
2000-09-09.abstract: Pres Clinton meets with Pres Jiang Zemin of China in New York City, in cordial session that, as expected, produces no breakthroughs; urges him to open dialogue with Taiwan's new government and to stop China's missile exports to Pakistan; photo (M)

2000-09-09.title: V.I.P.'s Are Helped Through Kennedy by a V.B.P. (Very Busy Person)
2000-09-09.abstract: Sgt Vito Asparro of Port Authority police, who is in charge of dignitary protection at Kennedy International Airport, is busy preparing for mass exodus of world leaders from United Nations Millennium Summit; photo (M)

2000-09-09.title: U.N. Meeting Ends With Declaration of Common Values
2000-09-09.abstract: UNITED NATIONS, Sept. 8 — Three days of brainstorming by the leaders of virtually all the world's nations ended tonight with a brave attempt to agree on a set of goals for the next millennium, and common values by which to govern.

2000-09-10.title: South Africa Warns Group After Fatal Attack on Judge
2000-09-10.abstract: PRETORIA, South Africa, Sept. 9 — South Africa's minister of justice, incensed by the assassination this week of a judge who has presided over an important terrorism trial in Cape Town, has said he will ban the organization suspected in the slaying if the group continues what authorities say is a two-year campaign of bombings and terror.

2000-09-10.title: Palestinians Meet on State
2000-09-10.abstract: JERUSALEM, Sept. 9 — A Palestinian leadership body opened a two- day, closed-door assembly in Gaza City today, at the end of which they are expected to defer the declaration of Palestinian statehood originally set for next week.

2000-09-10.title: Identity Crisis for Denmark: Are We Danes or Europeans?
2000-09-10.abstract: Denmark is experiencing acute identity crisis similar to several other European states; its recurring 'Hamlet syndrome' that something is rotten in state of Europe has reached new pitch as September 28 referendum nears on whether to preserve krone or follow 12 other states of 15-member European Union in abandoning local currencies for euro; situation has fed growing anxiety that Denmark may not be master of its own house anymore and reflects widespread concern across Europe over dilution of nationhood through European integration and mounting immigration; as country is only one that votes regularly on how far Europe should integrate, 'no' vote could further undermine currency whose value against dollar has plunged 25 percent in year, delaying possibility that Britain and Sweden may adopte euro, and exposing seemingly undemocratic nature of mainly German and French drive to create federation resembling United States of Europe; map; photos (M)

2000-09-10.title: Leader Moves To Dominate Civic Groups In Venezuela
2000-09-10.abstract: Venezuela's Pres Hugo Chavez, whose control of government is assured by landslide victory in recent election, moves to replace independent civic and labor groups with equivalents under control of 'revolutionary' movement he heads; his immediate target is main labor coalition, Venezuelan Workers' Confederation, which he threatens to demolish; accuses its leaders of being elites who have betrayed working class; organization is sympathetic to opposition Democratic Action Party and has organized numerous strikes and protests against Chavez (M)

2000-09-10.title: Among Unpaid Wages of a Revolution: Competing Claims on Land in Nicaragua
2000-09-10.abstract: More than decade after elections removed Sandinistas from power in Nicaragua thousands of Nicaraguans, as well as about 900 American citizens and even descendants of former dictator Anastasio Somoza, are grappling with legacy of Sandinista revolution, namely, confiscated land, homes and businesses that were distributed to peasants and party loyalists alike; while few expect to get back all they owned, even skeptics decry what they say is convoluted, lengthy administrative and legal process that too often result in paltry settlements paid in low-value government bonds; undoing tangle is proving vexing for government, which has little cash and many concerns about social unrest that could result from attempts to dislodge thousands of people who acquired land through urban development plans and agrarian reform laws that were legitimate at time; maps; photo (M)

2000-09-11.title: Lagos Journal; Nigeria Echoes to the Beat That Defied Tyrants
2000-09-11.abstract: Many people still drop in at simple two-story house in Lagos, Nigeria, that was home of late Fela Anikulapo-Kuti, King of Afrobeat and foe of Nigerian dictators, who lived there with his many wives, family and friends and threw best parties in all of Nigeria, and probably all of West Africa; map; photo (M)

2000-09-11.title: ASSEMBLY IN GAZA DEFERS DECLARING PALESTINIAN STATE
2000-09-11.abstract: Palestinian leadership assembly bows to international pressure, postponing declaration of statehood and recommitting to Middle East peace effort despite deeply deadlocked negotiations; casts decision as forward-thinking rather than as defensive step; adjourns for up to two months, entrusting statehood aspirations to Yasir Arafat and senior advisers; Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak welcomes delay, but says he has no illusions about securing final peace agreement (M)

2000-09-11.title: Corrections
2000-09-11.abstract: Correction of August 31 article about aides to Pres Richard M Nixon who expressed skepticism about assertions in new book that president took antiseizure drug Dilantin

2000-09-11.title: NEWS SUMMARY
2000-09-11.abstract: INTERNATIONAL   A3-12    Palestinians Postpone Independence Deadline  A Palestinian leadership assembly decided to postpone a declaration of statehood and recommit to the Middle East peace effort. The Palestinian Central Council backed down from its pledge to declare an independent Palestinian state no later than Sept. 13. It adjourned for up to two months, entrusting its statehood aspirations to Yasir Arafat, the Palestinian leader, and his advisers.   A1

2000-09-11.title: Corrections
2000-09-11.abstract: Correction of August 1 article about role played by Congressional candidates at Republican National Convention

2000-09-12.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-09-12.abstract: EUROPE    AUSTRIA: SANCTIONS DECISION DUE  A decision on whether European Union partners will lift diplomatic sanctions against Austria is expected soon, according to France's European affairs minister, Pierre Moscovici. A European Commission panel said last week that continuing the sanctions would be counterproductive.    Suzanne Daley (NYT)

2000-09-12.title: Arafat's Task: Negotiating Sacred Ground
2000-09-12.abstract: Senior Clinton administration officials provide details of recent meeting between Pres Clinton and Yasir Arafat in New York to aid badly flagging Middle East peace talks; say Arafat suggested that Arabs be given sovereignty over Temple Mount, holy site in Jerusalem that is sacred to both Jews and Muslims and has become make-or-break issue in talks; say Clinton explained that if Israel could not accept exclusive Palestinian sovereignty, neither would it accept sovereignty by group of Islamic countries; suggested that Arafat's solution met his own needs but not that of Israel; photo (M)

2000-09-12.title: Britons Running Out of Patience as Costly Gas Runs Out
2000-09-12.abstract: More than 1,000 gasoline stations across Britain close after groups of protestors, furious at high fuel prices, blockade oil refineries cutting off fuel deliveries and sending consumers rushing to pumps in mad race to fill cars before fuel ran out; protests echo similar demonstrations in Belgium, where truck drivers paralyzed center of Brussels; irate mood sweeps across Europe; groups in Ireland, Italy, Netherlands and Germany mount or prepare to mount own actions, emboldened by success of recent protests in France; chart; photo (M)

2000-09-12.title: OBITUARY : Paul Bianchini, 72, Pop Art Dealer, Dies
2000-09-12.abstract: SENS, France—  Paul Bianchini, 72, for decades a leading figure in the art worlds of New York and Paris who in the 1960s brought Pop Art to worldwide attention and later introduced a new wave of postwar American painters to Europe, died here last Tuesday of liver cancer, his widow said Friday.

2000-09-12.title: Iraq Won't Let Outside Experts Assess Sanctions' Impact on Lives
2000-09-12.abstract: UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan tells Security Council Iraq will not allow independent experts into country to assess living conditions, decade after economic sanctions were imposed; says Iraqis are also barring group of experts to devise ways to improve oil-for-food program, developed to soften human impact of sanctions; earlier refusal to allow entry of council's sanctions chairman, Amb Peter van Walsum of Netherlands, noted; diplomats and relief officials speculate government wants to control all contact between Iraqis and outsiders, as well as right to decide how aid is distributed and by whom and total control over money if cash is to be made available for local purchases (M)

2000-09-13.title: Annan Tells General Assembly to End Barriers
2000-09-13.abstract: Sec Gen Kofi Annan opens UN General Assembly with tough message charging that Assembly itself is more often than not the wall where innovative ideas crash; demands action on reorganizing and strengthening peacekeeping and enlarging Security Council to increase diversity and voice of developing world; his photo (S)

2000-09-13.title: Anti-Free-Trade Protests Disrupt Economic Forum in Melbourne
2000-09-13.abstract: World Economic Forum in Melbourne is disrupted by thousands of protesters who clash with Australian riot police and block conference site, as happened in Seattle last year (M)

2000-09-13.title: Europe Lifts Sanctions on Austria, but Vows Vigilance
2000-09-13.abstract: European Union lift sanctions against Austria, seven months after hurriedly imposing diplomatic freeze on Vienna for bringing rightist party into governing coalition; photo; European officials warn they remain deeply concerned about Jorg Haider's Freedom Party and vow 'particular vigilance' (M)

2000-09-13.title: A Synagogue Next Door To Auschwitz
2000-09-13.abstract: Synagogue opens near site of Auschwitz death camp in Poland for first time since World War II; photo; map; Jewish officials, Polish ministers, Catholic bishop and Jordanian prince gather to hear messages of reconciliation, including one from Pres Clinton; atmosphere of good will is somewhat marred by recent opening of large disco nearby, which Polish authorities say they cannot close because site is private property (M)

2000-09-13.title: 'A' Is for Arafat, 'B' Is for Bethlehem
2000-09-13.abstract: First official school texts written for and by Palestinians emphasize fighting Israel, which is not even identified on maps, refer to 'liberation armies' and 'Judaization' of Jerusalem, and mention neither terrorism nor Oslo peace efforts; photo (M)

2000-09-14.title: $33 Billion Stock Deal To Form a U.S. Giant In Investment Banking : J.P. Morgan Being Sold To Chase Manhattan
2000-09-14.abstract: NEW YORK—  Chase Manhattan Corp. said Wednesday that it would buy J.P. Morgan & Co. for about dollars 33 billion in stock, positioning two of the most venerable names in American commercial banking to compete with the world's biggest securities houses.

2000-09-14.title: Rome Journal; English Without Tears, Thanks to TV
2000-09-14.abstract: Italians are learning English language by watching episodes of American television program, Beverly Hills 90210; installments of 'MovieTalk,' CD-ROM's that convert popular American television shows into interactive English lessons, are being given away free by newsmagazine L'Espresso; it hopes to sell magazines by tapping into Italians' thirst to improve their English; Italy has one of lowest rates of English proficiency among European contries; photo (M)

2000-09-14.title: European Countries Resist Cutting Fuel Taxes
2000-09-14.abstract: Truckers and farmers around continental Europe continue their protests, but political leaders dug in their heels against cuts in gasoline tax; acts of defiance in Belgium and Germany described; photo (M)

2000-09-14.title: White House to Revisit Chile Files That C.I.A. Wants to Block
2000-09-14.abstract: White House orders new look at several hundred files that Central Intelligence Agency intends to withhold when it releases American documents about 1973 military coup in Chile; move appears to signal victory for Secretary of State Madeleine K Albright and other advocates of broad disclosure of American role in overthrow of Socialist government of Pres Salvador Allende and ensuing 17-year dictatorship of Gen Augusto Pinochet; George J Tenet, CIA director, contends that certain files must be withheld because they would compromiseintelligence techniques still in use (M)

2000-09-14.title: France Warns Iraq on Weapons Inspections
2000-09-14.abstract: French Foreign Min Hubert Vedrine, who has criticized sanctions against Iraq, nonetheless says he has warned Iraq's deputy prime minister, Tariq Aziz, not to expect any weakening of Security Council's determination to send United Nations inspectors back into Iraq to eliminate weapons of mass destruction before sanctions can be lifted (M)

2000-09-15.title: British Fuel Protest Halts, but Scarcity Of Gas Isn't Ended
2000-09-15.abstract: Blockade of British refineries and storage depots breaks up and oil trucks procede, unimpeded, for first time in week; Prime Min Tony Blair warns it will take some time before most drivers see gas back in their neighborhhd pumps; hastily organized protest of high fuel prices was instigated by Brynie Williams, who now urges other protesters to end their protests; Williams, Welsh farmer, says he feels siege should be lifted while it still occupies 'high moral ground,' and before disruptions erode popular support for cause; Blair reports that 12 of 20 main supply depots are operating freely (M)

2000-09-15.title: U.S. Moves to Revive Mideast Peace Talks but Plays Down the Prospects for Success
2000-09-15.abstract: Clinton administration moves to reopen peace talks between Israelis and Palestinians at informal working level in New York, but Secretary of State Madeleine K Albright plays down prospects that progress would be substantial enough to lead to accord in near future; Albright says Palestinian senior negotiators, Saed Erakat and Muhammad Dahlan, are meeting with Dennis B Ross, who handles Middle East negotiations for administration, and that she is meeting with Israeli foreign minister, Shlomo Ben Ami, separately (M)

2000-09-15.title: U.S. Puts Uzbek Group on Its Terror List
2000-09-15.abstract: Clinton administration adds Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan to its list of foreign terrorist organizations, saying group has ties to Saudi financier Osama bin Laden; decision reflects administration's determination to isolate groups it believes sponsor or abet international terrorism, especially those thought to be part of global network sponsored by bin Laden; addition of Islamic Movement to list of 28 foreign groups accused by Washington of sponsoring terrorism means American citizens and residents are legally barred from contributing money or 'material support' to group; designation sends signal to other states that US considers group dangerous, hoping they too will try to restrict its activities; map (M)

2000-09-15.title: Australian Cuisine Is a Delicious Mix : A Worldly Flavor Fusion
2000-09-15.abstract: ONCE A boring meat-and-potato country, Australia has witnessed a food revolution in the last 20 years as a unique blend of occidental and oriental techniques has created a truly Australian cuisine out of the riches of the country's seafood, meats and produce.

2000-09-15.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-09-15.abstract: ITALY: CARDINAL CALLED RACIST -- A leading cardinal has set off a political furor by suggesting that Italy favor Roman Catholic immigrants over Muslims to "preserve the identity of the nation." Many center-left government ministers condemned Cardinal Giacomo Biffi's pastoral letter to his Bologna archdiocese as dangerous and racist. But some center-right politicians agreed with his proposal and the opposition leader Silvio Berlusconi said the cardinal's ideas "were deemed worthy of attention."

2000-09-16.title: Nicaragua's Trade Zone: Battleground for Unions
2000-09-16.abstract: Local and international union rights groups see dismissals of workers fighting to unionize Taiwanese-owned clothing factory in Nicaragua as vital test for labor rights in foreign-owned factories in developing world's free-trade zones; fear poor nations will tolerate low wages, unsafe workplaces and union busting in name of free trade and foreign investment; photos (M)

2000-09-16.title: Albright Sits Face to Face With Iranian
2000-09-16.abstract: State Sec Madeleine K Albright and Iranian Min Kamal Kharazi find selves in rare accord in meeting at United Nations with senior diplomats from six other nations to discuss what to do about Afghanistan and its ruling Taliban; meeting is first at small forum by top foreign officials of Iran and United States since 1979; Sec Gen Kofi Annan presides; participants call for cease-fire in civil war (M)

2000-09-16.title: Suicide Bomber Kills 6 Others On Busy Street in Sri Lanka
2000-09-16.abstract: Tamil rebel suicide bomber kills self and six others, Colombo, Sri Lanka, in apparent effort to disrupt election campaigning; photo (M)

2000-09-16.title: Fuel Supplies Ease in Britain but Protests Spread on Continent
2000-09-16.abstract: Britain struggles to recover from paralyzing fuel blockades after weeklong truckers strike that saw 90 percent of filling stations go dry; rushes to replenish stocks for emergency services and public transportation; says it will be at least two eeks before supplies return to normal for ordinary drivers; rebound is swifter in Belgium, where impact was milder; truckers elsewhere in Europe step up pressure for relief from soaring gasoline and diesel prices (M)

2000-09-16.title: World Briefing
2000-09-16.abstract: EUROPE    RUSSIA: KURSK THEORIES -- The official inquiry into the sinking of the submarine Kursk has discarded 11 theories, leaving only the possibilities of an onboard accident and collisions with a World War II mine or another vessel, Deputy Prime Minister Ilya Klebanov told Parliament. Mr. Klebanov said that a Russian minisub would arrive at the wreck site on Sept. 23 or 24, and that the cause of the disaster might be known even before efforts begin to raise the sub or remove the remains of its 118 crewmen.   Michael Wines (NYT)

2000-09-17.title: Critic of Army Is Assassinated in Secessionist Region of Indonesia
2000-09-17.abstract: Tengku Safwan Idris, Islamic university rector and leading candidate for governor of Aceh, Indonesia, is assassinated at his home, Banda Aceh; was outspoken critic of use of military force to settle secessionist issue in province; police are unsure who is behind killing; secessionist leader Ayah Muni blames military; Hutomo Mandela Putra (Tommy Suharto) meets voluntarily with police and denies involvement in series of Jakarta bombings; map (M)

2000-09-17.title: New Subway For Istanbul Raises Specter Of Old Fears
Swiss to Vote On Plan to Set Limit on Influx Of Immigrants
2000-09-17.abstract: Switzerland to vote on proposal to restrict foreigners to 18 percent of population, now 7.3 million; nation, with largest number of foreigners per capita in Europe, is caught up in anti-foreigner sentiment; government is lobbying strongly against quota (M)/

2000-09-17.title: After Government Attack, Philippine Rebels Suggest New Talks
2000-09-17.abstract: JOLO, the Philippines, Sept. 17 — Pitched battles between government troops and Muslim rebels holding 19 hostages in the southern Philippines resumed at dawn today with ferocious mortar shelling of guerrilla positions, residents said.

2000-09-17.title: Venezuelan Is Rekindling Land Dispute With Guyana
2000-09-17.abstract: Venezuelan Pres Hugo Chavez, angered by Guyana's decision to let Beal Aerospace Technologies, American company, build satellite-launching center in territory claimed by Venezuela, revives long-dormant border dispute; holds space site would let United States establish military presence along Venezuela's vulnerable eastern flank; claims Guyana should cede some two-thirds of its 83,000 square miles; both Beal and Guyana deny site would be US military base; Chavez's critics, including Carlos Andres Perez, see effort to distract Venezuelans from mounting problems; map (M)

2000-09-18.title: UNCERTAIN TIMING FOR PERU ELECTION GIVES U.S. CONCERN
2000-09-18.abstract: Sudden decision by Peruvian Pres Alberto K Fujimori to call new elections from which he will withdraw as candidate surprises US and Latin America, which welcome move but express concern that nation faces period of unrest and voice uncertainty over what role army will play; thousands pour onto streets of Lima in jubilation; Fujimori meets with Cabinet but issues no public statements; decision to step down is sparked by public airing of embarrassing tape showing intelligence chief Vladimiro Montesinos apparently bribing opposition party member to switch to Fujimori's party prior to recent elections; opposition candidate Alejandro Toledo, who lost to Fujimori in runoff he boycotted, returns from US; expresses surprise over Fujimori decision; says he will attempt to rally splintered opposition for new elections; questions whether Fujimori's decision will stick; says he doubts Fujimori will keep word; Fujimori's rise to power over past decade and his entanglement in mounting corruption scandal discussed; increasing pressure from US and Organization of American States to enact democratic reforms since disputed re-election in May noted; photos (M)

2000-09-18.title: Metulla Journal; It's Raining Not Rockets but Rocks: This Is Peace?
2000-09-18.abstract: Israelis along unruly, 105-mile frontier with Lebanon are trying to adjust to life along border, ever since pullout of troops in May; normality has been elusive, even though Hezbollah has ceased rocket attacks that once drove residents of Metulla and Kiryat Shemona into underground shelters; Lebanese with festering animosities engendered by long Israeli occupation have found new ways to manifest anger; have resorted to stone-throwing, like young Palestinians during intifada, posing challenge to Israeli soldiers who are under orders not to retaliate unless Israeli lives are threatened; maps; photo (M)

2000-09-18.title: High-Tech M&A Boom Spurs Investment Banks
2000-09-18.abstract: PARIS—  One morning last January, several dozen men and women in suits gathered at the Marriott Hotel overlooking Times Square in New York City in a room reserved under the code name "Webgrinder."

2000-09-18.title: Cleric Uses Weapon of Religion Against Iran's Rulers
2000-09-18.abstract: Interview with Mohsen Kadivar, Islamic thinker and critic of Iranian government; former confidant of Pres Mohammad Khatami recently served 18-month prison sentence for allegedly spreading lies, defaming Islam and disturbing public opinion; photo (M)

2000-09-18.title: Freelance Consultants Tap Rich Services Niche
2000-09-18.abstract: FRANKFURT—  Until computers learn to fix themselves, Harry Scherbach will be one of the unsung heros of the new economy. His laidback, three-person business, which supports Macintosh users from a converted century-old bread factory in Frankfurt, has tapped into a boom niche for independent consultants who thrive in the shadow of the Internet.

2000-09-19.title: Cohen Warns Indonesians To Disband Timor Militias
2000-09-19.abstract: US Defense Sec William S Cohen warns Indonesia that troubled country will face severe diplomatic and economic reprisals if fails to disband militia groups accused of killings and of causing havoc in divided island of Timor; expresses grave disappointment in meetings with Pres Abdurrahman Wahid and other top officials that government has failed to disarm pro-Jakarta militia that is accused of killing three unarmed UN workers on September 6 in West Timor; photo (M)

2000-09-19.title: Hijacked Cuban Plane Crashes Off the Florida Keys
2000-09-19.abstract: Ahijacked Cuban plane with perhaps 18 people aboard crashed in international waters off the Florida Keys this morning, and Cuba asked for help in searching for survivors, United States officials said.

2000-09-19.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-09-19.abstract: EUROPE    YUGOSLAVIA: OFFER TO VOTERS  The European Union, in a policy coordinated with the United States, offered to lift sanctions on Serbia and provide aid if a candidate opposing President Slobodan Milosevic wins elections scheduled for Sunday and is allowed to take office. In a ''message to the Serbian people'' issued in Brussels, the Europeans urged Serbs to use their vote against Mr. Milosevic. On Saturday American officials made a similar offer. Steven Erlanger (NYT)    MONTENEGRO: MILOSEVIC AWAITED  President Slobodan Milosevic is expected to make a campaign visit to northern Montenegro tomorrow, said the Yugoslav prime minister, Momir Bulatovic. Mr. Bulatovic, left, heads the pro-Milosevic opposition party in Montenegro, whose government is urging its people to boycott the vote on Sunday and is edging toward independence from Serbia.    Steven Erlanger (NYT)

2000-09-19.title: Putin, Making a Gesture to Jews, Slips Into a Factional Morass
2000-09-19.abstract: Russian Pres Vladimir V Putin visits site of bombed-out Marina Roshcha synagogue in Moscow to dedicate $12 million Jewish community center; hails new structure as symbol of new era of tolerance in Russia; his strong gesture of support for faith that suffered decades of persecution during Soviet times places him in middle of bitter feud within Russia's reviving Jewish minority and raises questions among critics about his motives; has effectively given government blessing to Chabad Lubavich movement, Hasidic sect that runs synagogue and has been locked in murky feud with more mainstream Russian Jewish Congress since 1999, when it formed Federation of Jewisgh Communities as rival group; photo (M)

2000-09-19.title: Bomb Kills 16, Injures 80 in Pakistan Capital
2000-09-19.abstract: ISLAMABAD, Pakistan -- A powerful bomb placed in a crate of grapes ripped through a crowded market Tuesday morning, killing 16 people and wounding more than 80 in Pakistan's capital, police said. The blast was the deadliest of 40 bombings that have rocked Pakistani cities this year, and the government has blamed archrival India for many of the previous attacks. Pakistan's military ruler Gen. Pervez Musharraf condemned Tuesday's ``dastardly act of terrorism,'' but did not place blame. The explosion, the first in Islamabad this year, occurred while workers were unloading the crates from a truck, witnesses said. The blast triggered a stampede of workers and shoppers from the city's largest fruit and vegetable market. ``I heard the explosion and saw fruit flying in the air,'' said Hasan Khan, a shop owner who was just 20 yards from the blast. ``People started running and screaming. We pulled down the shutters on our shop and left.'' No group immediately claimed responsibility, and police were investigating. The truck arrived from Kurram Agency in Pakistan's northwestern tribal region bordering Afghanistan, police and witnesses said. The vegetable market is located on the city's outskirts in a relatively poor neighborhood. Many of the victims were Afghan refugees who work unloading trucks. Pakistan has been plagued by political violence, including periodic bomb blasts, for years. However, since Musharraf seized power last October, ousting an elected civilian government, overall political unrest has declined. And Islamabad has traditionally been calm even when other Pakistani cities were aflame.

2000-09-20.title: SENATE VOTES TO LIFT CURBS ON U.S. TRADE WITH BEIJING; STRONG BIPARTISAN SUPPORT
2000-09-20.abstract: Senate approves bill to end annual Congressional review of China's trade status and grant China permanent normal trade ties, 83-15; strong bipartisan support follows long and tumultuous struggle; Pres Clinton hails vote, which gives him what he views as key foreign policy goals; aide to Gov George W Bush hails vote; Vice Pres Al Gore, who feared losing support of labor unions who opposed bill, voices qualified support; Senate supporters hail deal, which will expand vast overseas market for American goods; photos (M)

2000-09-20.title: Vatican Looks to New Cardinals and Papabili
2000-09-20.abstract: Top Roman Catholic officials at Vatican seem to be working behind closed doors during Holy Year to reorganize top echelons and prepare for future; reassignment of Bishop Giovanni Battista Re, Pope John Paul II's deputy secretary of state, as head of office that selects bishops signals effort; he succeeds Cardinal Lucas Moreira Neves; Vatican is preoccupied with selection of up to 20 new cardinals, probably including Re, who could be both electors and potential papal candidates; photo (M)

2000-09-20.title: In Clinton´s Words: ´An Outstretched Hand´
2000-09-20.abstract: WASHINGTON, Sept. 19 — Following is President Clinton's statement today in the White House Briefing Room on the Senate vote on trade relations with China, as transcribed by the Federal News Service.

2000-09-20.title: Abbotts Barton Journal; Conservation Plan Rocks the Cradle of Fly-Fishing
2000-09-20.abstract: Anglers, who view stretch of River Itchen in Abbotts Barton, England, as cradle of fly-fishing, battle plan by local conservation agency to let part of fishery revert to nature; access to Itchen and other chalk-stream waters is largely restricted to sometimes snooty clubs and syndicates with high annual fees; map; photo (M)

2000-09-20.title: Suave Rival Has Words For the U.S.: 'En Garde'
2000-09-20.abstract: Hubert Vedrine, French foreign minister, discusses his new book on making France center of streamlined Europe that would be equal partner to currently dominant United States, interview (M)

2000-09-21.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-09-21.abstract: EUROPE    LUXEMBOURG: FUEL CONFERENCE  European Union transport ministers met for emergency talks to try to stop fuel price protests that have spread across the Continent in the past three weeks. The ministers were considering appealing to the Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries to produce more oil. They were also considering making a call for a unified fuel tax.   (AP)

2000-09-21.title: How 60 Israeli Women Managed to Dump the Boss
2000-09-21.abstract: Sixty immigrant women workers have succeeded in preventing closure of their Mizpe Ramon, Israel, garment factory by barricading selves in factory for month, attracting national attention; are set to become first textile cooperative in Israel, first all-female cooperative and first one in ailing immigrant development town; map; photo (M)

2000-09-21.title: 2 French Journalists Tell of Escape From Jolo Rebels
2000-09-21.abstract: ZAMBOANGA, Philippines—  After 10 weeks of captivity in the mosquito-invested hills of a remote southern Philippine island, freedom for two French journalists came during a moment of confusion.

2000-09-21.title: Iran´s Well-Covered Women Remodel a Part That Shows
2000-09-21.abstract: TEHRAN, Iran — The law in Iran requires women to cover their hair and to conceal the shape of their bodies in loose clothing.

2000-09-21.title: South Korean Aide Resigns Over Bank Loan Accusations
2000-09-21.abstract: Culture and Tourism Min Park Jie Won, close aide to South Korean Pres Kim Dae Jung, resigns after being accused of using his position to help businessmen get bank loans; he denies charge (M)

2000-09-22.title: Attack on MI6 Snarls Central London : Missile Hits the Home Of British Intelligence
2000-09-22.abstract: LONDON—  A small missile smashed into the eighth floor of the headquarters of MI6, Britain's foreign intelligence service, and the London Metropolitan Police warned Thursday of a "genuine threat of terrorism" in London.

2000-09-22.title: Mutilation Victims Aided By Swiss Surgical Team
2000-09-22.abstract: Doctors report, in British medical journal The Lancet, that little-known operation developed by German Army surgeon during World War I has been used to restore some grasping ability to people whose hands were chopped off with machetes in terrorist attacks during civil war in Sierra Leone; photo (M)

2000-09-22.title: Peru's Military Backs Fujimori in His Call for New Elections
2000-09-22.abstract: Peru's military leaders publicly back Pres Alberto K Fujimori, ending five days of silence during which there were widespread fears that armed forces might move to oust him (M)

2000-09-22.title: Pentagon Says North Korea Is Still a Dangerous Military Threat
2000-09-22.abstract: New Pentagon report concludes that, despite recent signs of diplomatic warming on Korean peninsula, North Korea remains a dangerous military threat, pouring its limited resources into buildup of conventional forces and development of short- and long-range missiles (M)

2000-09-22.title: Why Did French Runner Run Away?
2000-09-22.abstract: SYDNEY—  Under the gaze of 110,000 fans in the goldfish bowl of Olympic Stadium and a worldwide television audience of millions, Marie-Jose Perec, the French runner, was to begin her quest for a third consecutive gold medal in the 400 meters on Friday.

2000-09-23.title: U.S. Envoy to Israel Relieved of Post Over Security Issues
2000-09-23.abstract: Martin S Indyk, US ambassador to Israel and an architect of US Mideast policy, is temporarily relieved of post and has had security clearance lifted pending investigationg of whether he mishandled classified materials; State Department says there is no indication of espionage and no US intelligence was compromised; photo (S)

2000-09-23.title: Prague Journal; Protesters Assemble, Hoping for a Rerun of Seattle's Show
2000-09-23.abstract: Prague Journal on scene as anti-globalization protesters assemble for Group of Seven meeting, hoping to repeat demonstrations that marred World Trade Organization conference in Seattle last year; photo (S)

2000-09-23.title: Critics Press World Bank's President for More Reform
2000-09-23.abstract: PRAGUE—  CORRECTION PUBLISHED IN THE INTERNATIONAL HERALD TRIBUNE DATED September 25, 2000

2000-09-23.title: Fears Deepen Milosevic Will Rig Vote
2000-09-23.abstract: Yugoslavia prepares to vote, with opinion polls showing Pres Slobodan Milosevic trailing Vojislav Kostunica by margin that opposition fears could be overcome by fraud; photo; opposition warns Milosevic will attempt widespread vote manipulation, especially in Kosovo (M)

2000-09-23.title: U.S. and Allies Meet to Debate Global Economic Goals
2000-09-23.abstract: PRAGUE, Sept. 23 — The top United States financial officials, fresh from parallel plunges into world oil and currency markets, sought to rally allies in the Group of 7 industrialized nations today to fight what they labeled twin threats to the record- breaking American economic expansion.

2000-09-24.title: A Nod, a Smile, Trying to Decipher Iran's Message on Jailed Jews
2000-09-24.abstract: Confusion over indications from Min Kamal Kharazi, now denied by his spokesman, that Iran would free some of 10 Iranian Jews jailed for allegedly cooperating with Israel and sharply cut sentences of others underscores difficulty of communicating with Iran through third parties in absence of official dialogue with United States; also reflects personal negotiating style of Kharazi, whose speech is barely audible and who often answers questions with nod or monosyllable (M)

2000-09-24.title: China Clerics Deny Reports That Churches Are Bullied
2000-09-24.abstract: Leaders of China's official Protestant church defend China's policies on religious freedom, holding record 15 million Chinese now worship in approved churches and that persecution of so-called underground Christians is rare, news conference; charge meddling by hostile foreign evangelists has caused many of reported conflicts between Christians and police; news conference is tied to 50th anniversary celebration of movement that directs Protestant affairs in China (M)h

2000-09-24.title: Indonesian Police Report Arresting 25 Suspects in Jakarta Bombings
2000-09-24.abstract: JAKARTA, Indonesia, Sept. 24 -- The Indonesian police said today that they have arrested 25 people in connection with a recent series of bombings in Jakarta, including one that killed 15 people at the stock exchange.

2000-09-24.title: Canada Calls for Moratorium on Debt Repayments From Poor Countries
2000-09-24.abstract: PRAGUE, Sept. 24 -- Canada on Sunday called for a moratorium on debt repayments for poor countries that commit themselves to effective, corruption-free governance. Canadian Finance Minister Paul Martin, in a speech prepared for delivery here, told International Monetary Fund policymakers that a debt relief scheme they launched four years ago with the World Bank was moving too slowly. He recalled that the IMF last year had made a commitment to stepping up the pace of the heavily indebted poor countries initiative (HIPC), which offers relief to impoverished nations that adhere to IMF economic reforms and draft action plans to eliminate poverty. "We made a commitment last year to faster action and we must make good on this commitment," Martin told a meeting of the IMF's International Monetary and Financial Committee. "Too many heavily indebted countries have still not benefited from the faster, deeper and broader debt relief promised under the enhanced HIPC initiative ...We must ask ourselves whether we cannot do more. For example, why should we not consider an immediate moratorium on debt payments from eligible reforming HIPC countries?" He said the moratorium could be extended to countries "committed to principles of good governance and are not in conflict." But the actual cancellation of the debt would not take place until countries have been deemed finally eligible for relief under HIPC. Such an approach, according to Martin, would "create an incentive for countries to continue to implement good policies." Of some 40 countries that are in principle eligible for HIPC treatment, only 10 -- Benin, Bolivia, Burkina Faso, Honduras, Mali, Mauritania, Mozambique, Senegal, Tanzania and Uganda -- have actually been approved for debt relief. Canada is currently owed $1 billion from 17 countries.

2000-09-24.title: Rescue Attempt for the Euro Falls Short of Bankers' Hopes
2000-09-24.abstract: Plan by world's major central banks to rescue euro shows signs of being more modest and tentative than it first seemed; American officials have disappointed some European bankers by not speaking out robustly in support of stronger euro and have raised doubts about their willingness to intervene forcefully in currency markets; central bankers also make it clear that their intervention is over, at least for now (M)

2000-09-25.title: Swiss Voters Reject Limit On Number of Foreigners
2000-09-25.abstract: Swiss voters reject proposal to impose constitutional limit on percentage of foreigners in country, apparently heeding government warnings that it risked harming economy and ties with rest of Europe (S)

2000-09-25.title: Rich Nations and Some Oil Producers Call for Price Cuts
2000-09-25.abstract: Representatives of rich nations meeting in Prague, Czech Republic, claim breakthrough in battle against high oil prices after persuading several oil-producing countries to join them in calling for cheaper crude; Saudi Arabia, Algeria, Russia and United Arab Emirates join Group of 7 industrialized nations and other members of committee that governs International Monetary Fund in condemning current crude oil prices as harmful (M)

2000-09-25.title: Peru Spy Chief Flies to Panama Looking for Asylum
2000-09-25.abstract: Vladimiro Montesinos, disgraced spy chief who was until recently Peru's second most powerful figure, flies to Panama to request asylum in wake of bribery scandal that threatened to topple Pres Alberto K Fujimori; flight appears to be result of diplomatic intervention by US, other Latin American countries and Organization of American States; all have exerted pressure on Fujimori to introduce more democracy and have urged all sides to avoid violence; photo (M)

2000-09-25.title: Yugoslavia´s Opposition Leader Claims Victory Over Milosevic
2000-09-25.abstract: BELGRADE, Serbia, Sept. 25 — The opposition candidate in the Yugoslav presidential election claimed victory today over Slobodan Milosevic in elections on Sunday. But with official vote counting suspended, there were concerns about whether Mr. Milosevic is willing to acknowledge defeat.

2000-09-25.title: BOTH SIDES CLAIM TO HOLD THE LEAD IN YUGOSLAV VOTE
2000-09-25.abstract: Political opposition claims that Vojislav Kostunica leads Pres Slobodan Milosevic by wide margin in Yugoslavia's presidential election, but government spokesman says opposite is true; government officials who predicted victory for Milosevic in first round, with incumbent taking more than 50 percent of vote, admit privately that mood is grim and that there is confusion about how to respond to vote; second-round runoff between Kostunica and Milosevic seems likely; photos (M)

2000-09-26.title: U.S. Says Asylum in Panama Helped Avert a Coup in Peru
2000-09-26.abstract: US officials defend role in pressing Panama to give refuge to Vladimiro Montesinos, Peru's ousted intelligence chief, asserting that his departure was necessary to avoid coup by officers loyal to him; administration officials confirm Montesinos has had longstanding relationship with American intelligence agencies but say contacts were informal and had no bearing on efforts to help him obtain asylum (M)

2000-09-26.title: French Scandal Turns Bipartisan as Socialist Is Accused
2000-09-26.abstract: Former French Socialist finance minister Dominique Straus-Kahn, who is at center of financial scandal, acknowledges having for past two years copy of videotape that accuses Pres Jacques Chirac of illegal activities; admits that he did not notify authorities of its existence; former party official Jean-Claude Mery, who has since died of cancer, is heard describing in detail vast network of kickback schemes he oversaw that allegedly brought Chirac's party millions of dollars annually in 1980's; tape was made in 1996 when Mery was being investigated for illegal fund-raising; says he made tape to protect himself; Chirac denies accusations; calls for speedy investigation into whether Strauss-Kahn got potentially damaging tape from lawyer in exchange for giving huge tax break to one of lawyer's clients; photo (M)

2000-09-26.title: Germany Set To Overhaul Its Program On Pensions
2000-09-26.abstract: FRANKFURT—  The German government will unveil a long-awaited overhaul of the state-run retirement system Tuesday in the latest reform of its once-sacrosanct welfare state.

2000-09-26.title: 2 Korea Defense Chiefs Agree On Small Steps at Initial Talks
2000-09-26.abstract: Defense ministers from North and South Korea meet in Cheju, South Korea, to discuss lowering of tensions across most heavily armed border in world; agree to open demilitarized zone so workers can begin reconstructing several railroad links; photo (M)

2000-09-26.title: A Religious Revival Near an Israeli Jail
2000-09-26.abstract: Area outside Maasiyhu Prison in Ramie, Israel, where Sephardic leader Aryeh Deri has been imprisoned for corruption has mushroomed into pilgrimage site where hundreds, and sometimes thousands, of followers gather every night for Jewish equivalent of religious revival meeting; gatherings have taken on life of their own, drawing mostly Sephardic Jews of Middle Eastern and North African origin, many from economically depressed communities; map; photo (M)

2000-09-27.title: Libyan Double Agent Testifies in Lockerbie Bomb Trial
2000-09-27.abstract: Abdul Majid Giaka, Libyan double agent, testifies in Netherlands trial of Al Amin Khalifa Fhimah and Abdelbaset Ali Mohmed al-Megrahi in 1988 bombing of Pan Am Flight 10 over Lockerbie, Scotland (M)

2000-09-27.title: French Parties Spar in Fund-Raising Scandal
2000-09-27.abstract: Cooperation that has long marked relationship between Pres Jacques Chirac and Prime Min Lionel Jospin of France unravels as their supporters charge each other with corruption and political manipulation; growing scandal stems from videotaped confession by late Jean-Claude Mery, former official of Chirac's conservative party, charging Chirac ran vast illicit fund-raising scheme when he was Paris mayor and party head; engulfs Jospin with disclosure that his former finance minister, Dominique Strauss-Kahn, had tape for two years without turning it over to authorities; new evidence suggests Mery may have been paid off not to go public with charge; photos (M)

2000-09-27.title: Protests Distract Global Finance Meeting
2000-09-27.abstract: Masked protesters hurl cobblestones and attack police with homemade gasoline bombs in unsuccessful bid to shut down joint World Bank-International Monetary Fund meeting, Prague; riot police escort government ministers into special subway cars to whisk them from meetings at end of session, restoring normal access by evening; police and many protesters say squadrons of European anarchists and communists provoked violence; many of financial leaders pledge to rededicate selves to fighting poverty; World Bank president James D Wolfensohn cites need to make globalization instrument of opportunity and inclusion, not fear; police prepared for siege, with 11,000 officers outnumbering demonstrators; photos (M)

2000-09-27.title: After Pact on Rebuilt Railway, Two Koreas Plan More Talks
2000-09-27.abstract: CHEJU, South Korea, Sept. 26 — North and South Korea agreed today to a limited reopening of the demilitarized zone that separates the countries to repair a railway link that has been severed for more than 50 years.

2000-09-27.title: Sombre, Reflective Mood at IMF After Violent Clashes
2000-09-27.abstract: PRAGUE, Sept. 27 -- Violent street clashes cast a pall over sombre IMF meetings here on Wednesday as delegates met defiantly in public, but questioned privately if the costs of large-scale global gatherings were starting to get too big. Only a few dozen delegates were present for the formal start of the second day of annual meetings of the World Bank and International Monetary Fund, held in a city clearing up after a night of battles between police and anti-capitalism activists. South African Finance Minister Trevor Manuel opened events with a message of sympathy to injured police officers and one of thanks to the Czech authorities for keeping the meeting secure. ``We wish those injured police a speedy recovery,'' he said. Delegates said they were saddened by the trouble on the streets outside, but angry that a few hundred violent demonstrators seeking to abolish the bank and the fund had stolen the world's attention. ``Violence has no place in a civilised society and I stand here on behalf of India, the land of Mahatma Gandhi who espoused the cause of non-violence, and I condemn the violence which was unleashed here yesterday,'' Indian Finance Minister Yashwant Sinha told fellow delegates. ``Whether the IMF and the World Bank exist or do not exist is a matter which will be decided by the will of the 182 countries who are represented here, not by a handful of hoodlums in the streets of Prague.'' This year's annual meetings have attracted some 15,400 delegates, about 1,000 fewer than in 1999. The gatherings are traditionally held outside Washington every three years -- the previous out-of-town meeting was in Hong Kong and the next is scheduled for Dubai in 2003. HARD TO WORK Delegates agreed that the annual meetings were an important formal for bilateral talks as well as for set piece, formal sessions. But some admitted it was hard to work against a backdrop of protest and violence -- activists on Tuesday got to within metres (feet) of the edge of the final security zone, hurling eggs and cobblestones at police and delegates. ``Maybe we should look at holding these meetings once every two years -- but you can't quote me by name on that,'' said one blue-badged delegate, the symbol of a governor or official who is formally representing his country. ``You have to wonder if these meetings can be held from now on -- who could possibly want to host them?'' a delegate from northern Europe said. But Zdenek Hruby, the Czech official responsible for coordination with the bank and the fund, said the meetings were still profitable, despite the additional security costs. ``Of course I would recommend to any country to host this event for any country, because it makes many profits for the host country,'' he told a news conference in the Prague Congress Centre. ``But I would recommend not believing any promises from the organisers and coordinators of similar street demonstrations. I would recommend strict cooperation with security forces of neighbour countries to identify these people. They are...not protesters, they are criminals. People who throw stones at buses and at citizens are criminals. They are not civilised people.'' The protest organisers had promised to keep their demonstrations peaceful and police had clearly expected sit-down demonstrations rather than violent clashes. Officers often appeared nervous and ill-prepared in the face of angry, aggressive activists. RAMPAGE AND ARRESTS Hruby said over 500 people had been arrested after the violence, which included a firebomb attack on a group of police officers and a late-night rampage through Prague's central Wenceslas Square, the scene of peaceful protests which helped bring down Communism in 1989. Protest continued on Wednesday but on a much smaller scale. The groups say they are continuing a tradition started with protests which disrupted trade talks in Seattle last year and almost halted IMF meetings in Washington last April, but delegates were wary of their motives. ``Initially in Seattle there was a lot of sympathy for the protesters, but now there is a sneaking feeling these people have got an agenda which is no longer that of the third world,'' said James Jonah, finance minister of war-torn Sierra Leone. ``We cannot stop these meetings just because others have their own agenda. How can we deal with our creditors if we do not get a chance to talk to them?'' Delegates also defended the bank and the fund, which the protesters accuse of neglecting the needs of the poor, or making life worse by attaching tough conditions to their loans. ``I am not saying that these institutions are perfect,'' said Jean-Francois Giovanni, the deputy director of the Swiss development corporation. ``But if they were not there we could have a situation like in the 1930s with the total collapse of the world financial system.'' ``If there was no international cooperation and no international management, the Asian crisis would have been 10 times worse.'' An IMF board member said the fund needed to do more to get its message across. ``The IMF is a very disciplined institution with very capable people, but sometimes we are viewed from outside as arrogant and aloof. We have to be careful,'' he said.

2000-09-28.title: Danes to Vote on Adopting the Euro
2000-09-28.abstract: Denmark holds its fourth referendum in less than decade on what degree of integration with European Union country desires; at issue in latest vote is whether Denmark should follow 12 other states of 15-member Union and join shared European currency, the euro (M)

2000-09-28.title: Pierre Elliott Trudeau, Canadian Prime Minister, Dies at 80
2000-09-28.abstract: Pierre Elliott Trudeau, the long-serving Prime Minister of Canada who successfully defended his vision of federalism and a unified nation against the aroused forces of Quebec separatism, died today. He was 80 years old.

2000-09-28.title: Rio Journal; Scandal Rocks Soccer, Sending Brazil Into Shock
2000-09-28.abstract: Soccer-mad Brazil reels as coach of its World Cup team is accused of corruption and several star players are under investigation for tax evasion; Brazilian squad, which has lost only one qualifying match in entire 70-year history of World Cup, has lost to Paraguay 2-1 and to Chile 3-0 and tied with Colombia and Uruguay, results regarded in Brazil as shameful; Brazilians are calling for head of coach, Wanderley Luxemburgo da Silva, who has appeared on national television, pleading with public to 'let me do my job'; photo (M)

2000-09-28.title: Air France Sues Continental Over Crash of the Concorde
2000-09-28.abstract: Air France says it has filed lawsuit against Continental Airlines because it believes metal strip from Continental DC-10 led to crash of its Concorde jet in July; Air France statement says that under civil aviation legal code, airline is responsible for damage caused by any pieces that fall off its aircraft; industry experts say it is rare for airlines to sue each other, but somewhat logical in this case; Continental issues brief statement saying there is no proof that it is linked to crash, which killed 109 people on board and 5 on ground (M)

2000-09-28.title: Defense in Lockerbie Trial Undermines a Key Witness
2000-09-28.abstract: Defense lawyers in Lockerbie trial systematically tear apart testimony of prosecution's most important witness, Abdul Majid Giaka, Libyan double agent who has been in United States federal witness protection program for decade; Abdul Majid's tentative performance, coupled with his earlier failure to link two defendants firmly to bomb, appears to seriously undermine his usefulness to prosecution and its effort to prove behond reasonable doubt that two Libyan defendants, Abdelbaset Ali Mohmed al-Megrahi and Al Amin Khalifa Fhimah, blew up Pan Am 103 in 1988, killing 270 people (M)

2000-09-29.title: Danish Voters Say No to Euro; A Blow to Unity
2000-09-29.abstract: Denmark votes not to join Europe's common currency, the euro, undermining already fragile monetary experiment and turning its back on rapid European political integration championed by France and Germany; final results show 53.1 percent of electorate voting 'No' to euro and 46.9 percent in favor; result is severe blow to Social Democratic prime minister, Poul Nyrup Rasmussen, who warned that Danes should accept euro or face isolation; rejection seen as ominous warning to European leaders that they may have raced ahead of their electorates in adopting euro, pressing for intensified political cooperation needed to support currency and talking of European federalism; 11 states of 15-member European Union introduced euro last year without holding popular vote (M)

2000-09-29.title: German Upper House Finishes Move to Berlin
2000-09-29.abstract: BERLIN, Sept. 28 — Just in time for the 10th anniversary of German reunification, the upper house of Parliament reopened today in a renovated Baroque palace to complete the government's return to the prewar capital.

2000-09-29.title: Mexico's New Chief Wants the Truth
2000-09-29.abstract: Mexico's president-elect, Vicente Fox Quesada, promises to create 'transparency commission' when he takes office nine weeks from now; says commission, which would have to be approved by Congress, would make accounting of past, of Institutional Revolutionary Party's 71-year-long rule of country; says commission will confront Mexico's biggest unsolved mysteries, involving assassinations and murders and billions of dollars in unaccounted-for money; Fox's advisers say they cannot build public trust in government if past remains minefield of secrets and lies; photo (M)

2000-09-29.title: Fujimori in Surprise Visit to Washington
2000-09-29.abstract: Pres Alberto K Fujimori of Peru makes surprise visit to Washington to seek support from United States and inter-American countries and organizations for his plan to transfer power to elected successor; trip stuns Clinton administration officials, who last week worried that he might be ousted by military officers loyal to his fired intelligence chief, Vladimir Montesinos; Montesinos has taken refuge in Panama, but American officials say he remains capable of influencing events in Peru; say Fujimori, who spurned Organization of American States for months, now seeks its embrace to thwart political or military challenges at home as he arranges transition; photo (M)

2000-09-29.title: Tokyo Journal; A Demimonde Scrutinized After a Disappearance
2000-09-29.abstract: Tokyo's hostess clubs are under harsh spotlight in British press since sudden disappearance in July of Lucie Blackman, 22-year-old British woman who came to Tokyo to live hostess life; Blackman case highlights what some experts and hostesses themselves say is feature that sets Japanese society apart from much of West: for Japanese men, paying for sex and other entertainment by women is common, and carries no stigma; some hostesses say clubs exert pressure on them to date customers; photo (M)

2000-09-30.title: Canada's Many Factions Come Together to Mourn Trudeau
2000-09-30.abstract: Canadians suspend political and regional differences to mourn former Prime Min Pierre Elliott Trudeau's death at age 80; parliamentary sessions are suspended; Trudeau's body will lie in state in Parliament's Hall of Honor; state funeral is planned, Canada's first in almost decade (M)

2000-09-30.title: Milosevic Foes Stage Protests To Force Him To Concede
2000-09-30.abstract: Thousands of people across Serbia rally in protest against Pres Slobodan Milosevic as opposition leaders call on foreign governments, including Russia and Greece, to examine disputed results of presidential vote; opposition plans rolling series of rallies, demonstrations and strikes to force Milosevic to concede that Vojislav Kostunica won majority of vote; street demonstrations get off to slow start, with rally in Belgrade poorly attended; protests in opposition strongholds attract big crowds; photo (M)

2000-09-30.title: Turkish Journalist Cleared of Insulting Army
2000-09-30.abstract: Three-judge panel dismisses charges that Turkish journalist Nadire Mater insulted military in her book about war against Kurdish separatists; case has been closely watched by advocates of press freedom and human rights; book, oral history based on interviews with army veterans, many of them disillusioned with their role in fighting, was banned and confiscated in June 1999; judges also lift ban on book (M)

2000-09-30.title: More a Loop Than a Knot
2000-09-30.abstract: Danish rejection of Europe's common currency and increasing likelihood that Slobodan Milosevic will be ousted from power in Yugoslavia discussed as improving outlook for Europe of loosely associated democratic nations instead of federal Europe of united states; Milosevic's ouster would remove last major obstacle to Europe of democracies and market economies; Danish vote shows attachment to national identity is resurgent in some areas; photo (M)

2000-09-30.title: Palestinian, Israeli Forces Exchange Fire on Third Day of Clashes
2000-09-30.abstract: JERUSALEM, Sept. 30 -- Israeli troops and Palestinian police exchanged fire in a third day of clashes on Saturday, further damaging chances of securing an Arab-Israeli peace deal. About 200 Palestinians were wounded in the violence sweeping the West Bank and Gaza Strip, and the death toll rose to at least seven from violence earlier this week. It started after right-wing Israeli politician Ariel Sharon -- a figure of hatred for Palestinians -- visited a site in Jerusalem's Old City on Thursday that is sacred to both Jews and Muslims. Witnesses said Palestinian police and Israeli soldiers opened fire during clashes near a Jewish settlement in Gaza on Saturday, and medical sources said more than 70 Palestinians had been injured in the violence there. Israeli security forces also opened fire in the West Bank city of Nablus when hundreds of Palestinians marched towards an army checkpoint. Witnesses reported three people hurt.
